proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one feature in the dataset is associated with objective_response at p<0.05 in a univariate test (chi-square for categorical/binary features, Welch t-test for continuous features comparing responders vs. non-responders).

analyses.code: for col in df.columns: stats.chi2_contingency / stats.ttest_ind by feature type
analyses.result_summary: Of 123 features tested, 14 reached p<0.05. Strongest associations: feature_051 (continuous/ordinal 0-2, p=1.1e-99), feature_011 (continuous 0-28, p=2.8e-35), feature_013 (binary, p=1.1e-33), feature_067 (binary, p=2.0e-18), feature_006 (binary, p=1.0e-6). Overall ORR is 16.89% (8447/50000).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Increasing values of feature_051 (ordinal 0/1/2; resembles ECOG performance status) are associated with monotonically lower objective_response rate.
Increasing values of feature_011 (continuous, range 0-28; resembles a comorbidity or pack-year burden index) are associated with monotonically lower objective_response rate.
Patients with feature_013==1 have lower objective_response rate than those with feature_013==0.
Patients with feature_067==1 have lower objective_response rate than those with feature_067==0.
Patients with feature_006==1 have higher objective_response rate than those with feature_006==0 (suggesting feature_006 may be an effective therapy or favorable biomarker).
Higher values of feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5; resembles serum albumin g/dL) are associated with higher objective_response rate.
Higher values of feature_063 (right-skewed continuous, range 0.03-296.88; resembles LDH or an inflammatory marker) are associated with lower objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by feature_051: 0->21.1% (n=17557), 1->15.7% (n=24962), 2->11.1% (n=7481). Strict monotonic decrease. Chi-square p=2.9e-93. Absolute ORR gap between PS 0 and PS 2 is 10.0 percentage points.

ORR by feature_011 quintile: Q1 18.9%, Q2 17.0%, Q3 15.8%, Q4 13.7%. Strict monotonic decrease across quintiles. Welch t-test p=2.8e-35; mean of feature_011 is 0.55 lower in responders (3.38) than non-responders (3.93).
ORR with feature_013==1: 15.40% (n=32365); ORR with feature_013==0: 19.64% (n=17635). Difference -4.25 pp; chi-square p=1.1e-33.
ORR with feature_067==1: 14.36% (n=12584); ORR with feature_067==0: 17.75% (n=37416). Difference -3.39 pp; chi-square p=2.0e-18.
ORR with feature_006==1: 18.19% (n=14258); ORR with feature_006==0: 16.38% (n=35742). Difference +1.82 pp; chi-square p=1.0e-6.
ORR by feature_099 quintile: 15.9%, 16.5%, 17.2%, 17.3%, 17.9%. Monotonic increase. Welch t-test p=1.3e-4; mean feature_099 0.023 higher in responders (3.82) than non-responders (3.80).
ORR by feature_063 quintile: 18.2%, 17.0%, 16.9%, 16.6%, 15.7%. Monotonic decrease. Welch t-test p=4.2e-4; mean feature_063 0.37 lower in responders (5.78) than non-responders (6.15).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable logistic model, feature_051 (PS-like), feature_011, feature_013, feature_067 each retain a significant negative association with objective_response, and feature_006, feature_099, feature_007, feature_039, feature_092 each retain a significant positive association with objective_response.
Patient age (feature_078) is not independently associated with objective_response after adjustment for performance status, comorbidity burden, treatment, and laboratory features.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_051 + feature_011 + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_006 + feature_099 + feature_063 + feature_007 + feature_039 + feature_092 + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + C(feature_043)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression (n=50000): feature_051 OR 0.683 (p=1.3e-95, negative); feature_011 OR 0.961 per unit (p=1.9e-33, negative); feature_013 OR 0.739 (p=1.0e-34, negative); feature_067 OR 0.772 (p=5.1e-19, negative); feature_006 OR 1.139 (p=1.9e-6, positive); feature_099 OR 1.097 (p=1.3e-4, positive); feature_063 OR 0.995 per unit (p=4.7e-4, negative); feature_007 OR 1.085 (p=7.6e-4, positive); feature_039 OR 1.079 (p=1.6e-3, positive); feature_092 OR 1.189 per unit (p=2.4e-3, positive); feature_078 (age) OR 1.0003 per year (p=0.79, NS); histology and smoking categorical effects NS. All effects retain direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between feature_006 (probable therapy) and feature_092 (continuous, range 0-0.8; resembles a fractional biomarker such as PD-L1 expression) on the log-odds scale: i.e., the response benefit of feature_006 increases as feature_092 increases.
The effect of feature_006 on objective_response differs by tumor histology (feature_043 adenocarcinoma vs. squamous).
The effect of feature_006 on objective_response differs by performance status (feature_051).
There is a positive interaction between feature_013 and feature_092 on the log-odds of objective_response.
feature_006 and feature_013 show a multiplicative interaction (i.e., the joint effect of receiving both differs from the product of their individual effects).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_006 * feature_092 + feature_051 + feature_011 + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_099 + feature_063', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_006:feature_092 interaction coef = +0.5946 (OR 1.81 per unit of feature_092 added when feature_006==1), p=1.5e-6. Main effect of feature_006 alone (when feature_092=0) becomes non-significant (coef -0.058, p=0.22), and main effect of feature_092 alone (when feature_006=0) is also NS (p=0.92). The treatment-biomarker synergy is robust.
feature_006:squamous interaction coef = -0.024, p=0.671. No evidence the feature_006 benefit differs between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology.

feature_006:feature_051 interaction coef = +0.040, p=0.316. No significant modification of the feature_006 effect by performance status; feature_006 appears to provide a similar relative benefit across PS levels.

feature_013:feature_092 interaction coef = +0.245, p=0.035. Weakly significant positive interaction; among feature_013==1 patients there appears to be some additional response gain with higher feature_092, but the effect is much smaller than for feature_006.
feature_006:feature_013 interaction coef = -0.071, p=0.182. No significant interaction; effects of feature_006 and feature_013 act independently on the log-odds scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tumor histology (feature_043 squamous vs. adenocarcinoma) is associated with objective_response in unadjusted analysis.
Smoking status (feature_057: current/former/never) is associated with objective_response in unadjusted analysis.
feature_006 confers an absolute ORR benefit in every tumor histology subgroup (adenocarcinoma and squamous).
feature_006 confers an absolute ORR benefit in every smoking subgroup (current, former, never).

analyses.result_summary: ORR adenocarcinoma 16.71% vs squamous 17.37%. chi-square p=0.078. No significant unadjusted association between histology and ORR.
ORR current 17.23%, former 16.85%, never 16.38%. chi-square p=0.27. No significant unadjusted association between smoking status and ORR.

analyses.code: for hist in df['feature_043'].unique(): rate diff and chi2 within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Adenocarcinoma (n=36099): ORR f006=1 18.1% vs f006=0 16.2%, diff +1.9 pp, p<1e-4. Squamous (n=13901): ORR f006=1 18.4% vs f006=0 16.9%, diff +1.5 pp, p=0.026. Direction consistent in both histologic subgroups.

analyses.code: for sm in df['feature_057'].unique(): rate diff and chi2 within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Former smokers (n=27567): diff +1.5 pp, p=0.003. Current (n=14974): diff +2.0 pp, p=0.002. Never (n=7459): diff +2.8 pp, p=0.089 (NS due to smaller n). Direction consistent across all smoking strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_123) is associated with objective_response in unadjusted analysis, with non-white patients having different ORR than white patients.
Insurance type (feature_005) is associated with objective_response, with privately-insured patients having higher ORR than Medicaid patients after adjustment for clinical features.
Receipt of feature_006 (probable therapy) is approximately equal across racial and insurance subgroups (i.e., no major treatment access disparity).
After full adjustment for clinical features, race (feature_123) does not have a significant independent association with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by race: white 16.5%, hispanic 17.2%, black 17.8%, asian 17.7%, other 18.5%. chi-square p=0.045 (borderline). White patients have the lowest unadjusted ORR.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_006 + C(feature_005) + feature_051 + feature_011 + feature_013 + feature_067', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted for clinical features, private insurance vs. medicaid (reference) coef = +0.088, OR 1.092, p=0.020 (significant). Medicare vs. medicaid OR 1.064, p=0.099. Uninsured vs. medicaid OR 1.083, p=0.244. Unadjusted association was NS (p=0.23) but a small private-vs-medicaid disparity emerges after clinical adjustment.

Receipt of feature_006 by race ranges 28.2-30.4% (asian 28.8%, black 28.2%, hispanic 28.3%, other 30.4%, white 28.5%). By insurance: medicaid 28.9%, medicare 28.8%, private 28.2%, uninsured 27.4%. Differences are small (<3 pp). No major access disparity in this cohort.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_006 + C(feature_123) + clinical adj', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjusting for feature_006, feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, no race coefficient reaches p<0.05 (white reference: black p=0.97, hispanic p=0.47, other p=0.65, asian implicit reference). The borderline univariate race signal is not independent of clinical confounders.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063 (right-skewed continuous, possibly LDH) shows a significant negative association with objective_response when modeled as log(feature_063), supporting its plausible interpretation as an inflammatory/tumor burden marker.
feature_011 shows a monotonic dose-response decrease in ORR across deciles (no clear threshold or plateau).
Among other binary features (prevalence 5-50%) not yet examined, feature_007 retains an independent positive association with objective_response after adjustment for the established predictors.
Among other binary features (prevalence 5-50%) not yet examined, feature_112 has a negative independent association with objective_response after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: log(feature_063) coef = -0.0556, p=5.0e-7 in adjusted model. The log transform yields a stronger relationship than the linear form (which gave coef -0.005, p=4.7e-4), consistent with a multiplicative dose-response (the ORR decrease per doubling of feature_063 is ~3.8%).

ORR by feature_011 octile decreases monotonically: 19.2% -> 18.2% -> 16.7% -> 17.2% -> 15.9% -> 15.7% -> 14.3% -> 13.2%. ~6 pp absolute drop from lowest to highest octile.  No threshold; appears approximately log-linear.
Adjusted for feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_099, feature_063: feature_007 coef +0.082, OR 1.085, p=7.3e-4. Confirmed independent positive association.
Adjusted: feature_112 coef -0.101, OR 0.904, p=0.029. Significant independent negative association of small magnitude. (Prevalence 7.8% in cohort.)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the highest quartile of feature_092, feature_006==1 produces a clinically meaningful absolute ORR increase compared with feature_006==0, while in the lowest quartile of feature_092 feature_006 produces no detectable benefit (i.e., feature_092 is a predictive biomarker for the feature_006 effect).
Performance status (feature_051) does not modify the relative effect of feature_006 on objective_response (no interaction).
Performance status (feature_051) does not modify the slope of feature_011 on objective_response (no interaction).
feature_013 and feature_067 (both negative predictors) act independently on the log-odds scale (no significant interaction).

analyses.code: for q in feature_092 quartiles: ORR by feature_006 within quartile
analyses.result_summary: feature_092 quartile-stratified feature_006 effect: Q1 ORR diff +0.5 pp (p=0.53, NS); Q2 +0.8 pp (p=0.31, NS); Q3 +1.2 pp (p=0.10, NS); Q4 +4.7 pp (p=6.4e-10, highly significant, ORR rises from 16.4% without feature_006 to 21.1% with feature_006). Strong evidence feature_092 is a predictive biomarker for the feature_006 benefit.

PS x feature_006 interaction coefficients (vs PS=0 reference): PS=1 x feature_006 coef +0.061 (p=0.27); PS=2 x feature_006 coef +0.061 (p=0.50). Both NS; no qualitative or statistical evidence of effect modification by performance status.
PS x feature_011 interaction coefficients (vs PS=0 reference): PS=1 x feature_011 coef -0.011 (p=0.12); PS=2 x feature_011 coef +0.002 (p=0.85). Both NS.
feature_013 x feature_067 interaction coef +0.036, p=0.54. NS. The two negative predictors operate as additive (on log-odds) independent main effects with no synergy or antagonism.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A final multivariable logistic model with the feature_006 x feature_092 interaction, the seven main-effect clinical predictors (feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_099, feature_063, feature_007, feature_039), age, histology, smoking, race, and insurance reproduces the predictive-biomarker pattern: predicted ORR is essentially identical for treated vs. untreated patients at low feature_092 but rises ~6 percentage points higher for treated patients at high feature_092.
In the fully adjusted model, the private-insurance vs. medicaid disparity (private patients have higher adjusted ORR) persists.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_006 * feature_092 + feature_051 + feature_011 + feature_013 + feature_067 + feature_099 + feature_063 + feature_007 + feature_039 + feature_078 + C(feature_043) + C(feature_057) + C(feature_005) + C(feature_123)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final model n=50000, pseudo-R2=0.021, LLR p=1.4e-183. feature_006:feature_092 interaction OR 1.81 (95% CI 1.42-2.30, p=1.6e-6). Predicted ORR (other covariates at population/reference values): at feature_092=0.05, untreated ORR 15.4% / treated 15.0% (essentially identical); at feature_092=0.75, untreated 15.3% / treated 21.0% (+5.7 pp). Pattern of predictive biomarker strongly confirmed.

analyses.code: see final model coefficient for C(feature_005)[T.private]
analyses.result_summary: In the fully adjusted model, private vs. medicaid OR 1.093 (95% CI 1.014-1.177, p=0.020). Medicare OR 1.064 (p=0.098). Uninsured OR 1.086 (p=0.226). Disparity by insurance class persists after full adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Age (feature_078) is independent of objective_response in the fully adjusted model (no association).
Tumor histology (feature_043 adenocarcinoma vs squamous) is independent of objective_response in the fully adjusted model.
Smoking status (feature_057) is independent of objective_response in the fully adjusted model.

analyses.result_summary: feature_078 (age) coef +0.0003, OR 1.0003 per year, p=0.785. No independent age effect after adjustment.
Squamous vs. adenocarcinoma OR 1.041, p=0.153. No independent histology effect.
Former vs current OR 1.012 (p=0.67); Never vs current OR 1.013 (p=0.75). No independent smoking effect.
